daily_brief: The US-Israel war on Iran dominated headlines today as President Trump announced the successful rescue of a downed F-15 airman in what he called one of the 'most daring' operations in US military history [France 24, Globe and Mail, ABC Australia]. The rescue came amid continued Iranian missile and drone attacks on Israel, Kuwait, and Gulf states, with Iran's Revolutionary Guards claiming to have destroyed US aircraft during the operation [The Hindu]. Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Tehran, threatening 'all hell' if Iran does not make a deal or reopen the Strait of Hormuz [France 24]. The economic fallout continues to ripple globally, with five EU finance ministers calling for windfall profit taxes on energy companies [RFI], and a major African Union report warning the conflict poses 'serious risk' to African economies through higher fuel and food prices [RFI]. In other developments, Germany's new military travel restrictions for males under 45 drew attention as Europe bolsters defenses [BBC], while China executed a French national for drug trafficking despite diplomatic efforts, prompting condemnation from Paris [France 24]. The humanitarian toll of the Ukraine conflict also continued, with a Russian drone attack killing five at a Ukrainian market [BBC].

top_stories.headline: US Rescues Downed F-15 Airman in 'Most Daring' Iran Operation as Trump Issues 48-Hour Ultimatum
bullets: US forces rescued the second crew member of an F-15 Strike Eagle shot down over Iran on Friday, with Trump calling it one of the most daring search-and-rescue operations in US military history
Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed to have destroyed 'enemy flying objects' including a US C-130 aircraft during the rescue operation in central Iran
Trump reiterated his demand that Iran make a deal or reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours, warning 'all hell' would follow
Iran launched missiles and drones at Israel and Kuwait early Sunday, while Hezbollah claimed to have targeted an Israeli warship with a cruise missile

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's ultimatum and provided detailed liveblog coverage of regional attacks

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on the rescue operation and broader war context, included opinion pieces critical of US strategy

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on rescue, included Iranian claims about downed US aircraft

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iranian perspective and warnings about escalation

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage emphasizing Trump's praise for the operation

top_stories.headline: EU Finance Ministers Demand Windfall Tax on Energy Companies Amid Iran War Price Shock
bullets: Finance ministers of Germany, Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Austria jointly called for an EU-wide tax on energy company windfall profits in a letter to the European Commission
The ministers cited market distortions similar to the 2022 energy crisis following Russia's Ukraine invasion, requesting swift action
Oil and gas prices have spiked since US-Israeli strikes on Iran began February 28, threatening a new cost-of-living crisis across Europe
The letter emphasized that those profiting from the war's consequences 'must do their part to ease the burden on the general public'

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Reported factually on the ministerial letter and its context within broader energy market disruptions

top_stories.headline: Middle East War Threatens African Economies Through Trade Disruption and Rising Costs
bullets: A joint African Union and African Development Bank report warns the Iran conflict 'presents a serious risk to Africa' through higher fuel and food prices
The Middle East accounts for 15.8% of Africa's imports and 10.9% of its exports, making the continent vulnerable to supply chain disruptions
The report projects a 0.2 percentage point loss in Africa's GDP growth if the conflict exceeds six months
Reduced LNG deliveries from the Gulf will impact fertilizer production, threatening the crucial planting period through May

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage of the AU-AfDB report with specific economic projections

top_stories.headline: Germany Requires Military Approval for Males Under 45 for Extended Foreign Stays
bullets: Under Germany's Military Service Modernisation Act effective January 1, males aged 17-45 must obtain prior approval for stays abroad exceeding three months
The regulation aims to maintain a 'reliable and meaningful military registration system' in response to threats from Russia following the Ukraine invasion
The requirement went largely unnoticed until reported by Frankfurter Rundschau; the defense ministry acknowledged consequences could be 'far-reaching'
Regulations on exemptions are being developed to 'avoid unnecessary bureaucracy,' though enforcement mechanisms remain unclear

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced reporting including ministry statements and acknowledgment of potential impacts on young people

top_stories.headline: Russian Drone Attack Kills Five at Ukrainian Market as Cross-Border Strikes Intensify
bullets: A Russian drone struck a market in Nikopol, southern Ukraine, killing five people and injuring 21, including a 14-year-old girl
The attack follows at least 15 civilian deaths from drone and missile strikes across Ukraine on Friday, with Russia launching nearly 300 drones overnight
Ukraine retaliated with drone and missile attacks on the Russian city of Taganrog, killing at least one person and damaging an oil pipeline near the Baltic port of Primorsk
Ukrainian prosecutors are investigating the market attack as a war crime

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed casualty reporting with context on Nikopol's frequent targeting

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage focused on pipeline damage from Ukrainian strikes on Russia

additional_stories.headline: China executes French national convicted for drug trafficking in 2010 despite diplomatic efforts for clemency
US arrests niece and grandniece of slain Iranian General Qassem Soleimani after revoking their green cards
Burkina Faso's military ruler declares democracy 'is not for us' and tells citizens to forget about elections
Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement, with 12 deportees of undisclosed nationalities
Iraq resumes oil exports through Turkish pipeline as Middle East war reshapes energy transit routes
French and Japanese vessels cross Strait of Hormuz for first time since war began, using Iranian-approved route
US satellite firm Planet Labs announces indefinite blackout on imagery of Iran conflict zone at government request
Canada signs agreement with China to deepen financial sector ties during Finance Minister's Beijing visit
Pope Leo XIV celebrates first Easter vigil, calling for peace in a world torn by wars
UN adopts resolution recognizing transatlantic slave trade as gravest crime against humanity; France abstains
